top_insights: Competition is a lie; the real leverage is building what others ignore.
If you don't design your routine, life will assign you a worse one.
AI won't replace creators; it will replace those who refuse to build.
True wealth isn't a salary; it's owning the factory that solves the problem.
Storytelling isn't marketing fluff; it is the only asset that scales infinitely.
hook_patterns: Specific financial or status signals (e.g., '$5,000 hit', '9m subs')
Authoritative insights using historical figures or data points
angles: Deconstructing popular myths with specific, data-backed counter-examples
Showcasing the 'behind-the-scenes' reality of high-status success
Providing actionable, no-fluff frameworks for self-mastery and business growth
emotional_drivers: Validation of existing beliefs or status
Curiosity regarding hidden truths or 'big lies'
emerging_narrative: Audiences are resonating with authentic, high-leverage stories that cut through noise. There is a strong preference for content that validates the user's desire for autonomy, financial independence, and the belief that individual action (like building a factory or writing a book) can solve systemic problems better than established institutions.

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: “Cha-ching” $5,000 hit my PayPal account. “This has to be a typo...” I thought to myself. So I messaged him. “Hey, did you mean to send $5,000?” His response: “Yeah, is that enough?” And just like that, Ghostwriting completely changed my life trajectory. Here’s the story

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: Steve Jobs once said: "The most powerful person is the storyteller." And luckily, storytelling is a skill. But most people have never tried to learn – because no one showed them where to start. So here are 6 of the best storytelling threads (so you can start learning today):

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: The single most powerful habit for personal growth: Journaling. Over the past 5 years, I've journaled every single morning—and along the way, I've: •Answered over 1,000 questions •Tested every app, pen, & notebook But I always return to a pen, paper, and these 5 prompts:

viral_examples.author: paulg
viral_examples.text: Every picture of JP Morgan published in his lifetime (and thus nearly every picture published since) was heavily retouched. This is what he actually looked like.

viral_examples.author: gregisenberg
viral_examples.text: this youtuber with 9m subs saw his wife struggle to find a quality wheelchair — $5k price tags, long waits, made in china so he built his own factory in the USA now, he sells these $1,000 custom wheelchairs, shipped in weeks we need more creator-led businesses like this.

winning_patterns: Specific financial or status signals (e.g., '$5,000 hit', '9m subs')
Authoritative insights using historical figures or data points
